text: We might be able to predict the next Minecraft mob because of the golden dandelion. When I interviewed Mojang, I asked how much they look to bring back old forgotten mobs like they did with the zombie horse and they said they always look to the past and then mentioned the copper golem and how it was brought in even after losing a mob vote. And there's another popular mob that lost a mob vote that just makes sense, the Moo Bloom. It was supposed to spawn in the flower forest and would perfectly fit this update's cute theme and could grow golden dandelions on its

The silence armor trim is the rarest in Minecraft, spawning in just 1% of ancient city chests. But since you can then duplicate it, if you want the biggest flexes armor, you need the next three rarest. After my calculations, that's the spire armor trim from End Cities, the Vex armor trim from Woodland Mansions, and the bolt armor trim, a 6% drop chance from Vaults in the Trial Chambers. Combine these with Netherite armor and a Netherite trim, and you have one of the biggest flexes on you at all times.

If your elytra breaks as you're flying, you'll enter a freef fall to your death. But if you're conscious of your durability, you can take it off right before it breaks and put it back on just before you die. But elytra durability only goes down one for every second you fly. Meaning if you swap your elytra and chest plate quickly every second, you lose no durability and can extend your flight. And that's unenchanted. For each level of unbreaking, you gain 1 second of flight. Meaning with Unbreaking 3, it only loses durability every 4 seconds, which isn't even that

If you have rails set up like this, the connecting rail will always go this way every single time except for when it goes the other way. That's because it's not a left right thing. The rail will always bend to the east over the west or the south over the north due to biases in the game's code coined the southeast rule. But it's actually sometimes useful. TNT cannons will fire further facing south or east and actually significantly. From my testing, a basic TNT cannon went 11% further. That was 15 blocks just from facing

Minecraft just changed the baby zombie texture again. This was the first new one we got a couple weeks ago, and I had a problem with its hitbox not allowing it to go through oneb block gaps, but they since fixed it, and I was happy with it. But a lot of people hated this texture, and I just never really understood why. To me, it's pretty close to the old one, and I like the big head. But since everyone complained, we now have this one, which I'm seeing a lot of praise for, but I think it's super ugly. This is like the only new baby I actively dislike. So, I don't know. What team are you guys on?

If you want to find cherrywood, just look around the plains biome in areas with more hills and elevation. Any place where a meadow or snowy mountain intersects with the plains, there's a good chance for cherry trees. If you want to find mangrove wood, look around warm biomes like the desert, savannah, and jungle. And they almost always spawn next to an ocean, which will be warm or lukewarm. If you want to find pale oak wood, it always spawns touching a dark forest and in places where there's more elevation, similar to the cherry grove. So look for hells.

But it begs the question, how new is new? Cuz okay, golden dandelion, definitely new. Netherite horse armor, new. Happy ghast, I'd still say new. The creaking, I still feel like it's pretty new. The trial chambers and mace, I mean to casual players, I'd say new. I feel like it gets iffy around 1.20. 20, but I could still see calling it new. 1.19 in the deep dark is where I think I'd stop, but the hard cut off is 1.18, the new caves, cuz before that it feels

Besides the mushroom island, the deep dark [music] is the only biome in Minecraft where hostile mobs can't spawn besides the warden. But if you just break all the skullk shriers in an area, it becomes literally the safest place in the entire game because you can leave the normal skullk sensors here and any other players will still assume it's not safe and avoid it, making it a great place for hiding stuff. This could mean a secret base since [music] you're far enough from the surface where no one can see your name tag, or just a loot stash. You can put chests under a lava pool and they're [music] completely invisible.

Minecraft YouTubers used to be called like the diamond minecart or popular MMOs. Now we got dudes with names like Plurve and Blurfs. Oh my god, they weren't kidding when they said every Minecraft YouTuber is named some shoot like Blur or Zer nowadays. Every Minecraft YouTuber now is called some shoot like Tinky Bippo or Dorg. And they all talk the same exact way. Now I've addressed the blob skin epidemic. A yearly reminder I made mine before it was cool. But now we're facing the weird short username epidemic. And I think it's for two reasons. First, it's good for branding. Having numbers in your username isn't really good for that. And second, people like getting these short Minecraft names. And since most things are taken, you end up with this jumble of letters and then that becomes their YouTube name because they don't want to end up with this at the end of their username.

Vertical slabs may come to Minecraft. Jeb was asked on Blue Sky about vertical slabs and why they were denied for inhibiting creativity, in which Jeb replied, "I don't know why people say inhibited [music] creativity. That seems like some kind of meme people love to repeat. Last time we seriously discussed this topic, the team argued against it because of UI/Ux [music] and tech considerations." To which someone responded that the reason comes from minecraft.net net where Mojang said vertical slabs inhibit creativity. So vertical slabs don't exist because of user interface and experience. But I guess that means they [music] could actually come one

Since it just came out, I have the chance to get the speedrun record for obtaining a golden dandelion. I need three things. A dandelion, eight gold nuggets, or one gold ingot, and a crafting table, which is really just one log. But mining for gold would take a long time. So, luckily, it spawns as loot in various structures, most easily in ruined portals, shipwrecks, and certain village houses. So, after generating hundreds of worlds, this happened. I spawned in and immediately mined a log. I looked around and found this ocean before I caught a dandelion in the corner of my eye. I mined it, made a crafting table, and headed to the ocean to look for a shipwreck when I found this ruined portal, which had gold that I crafted into a golden dandelion in 40 Second.

This is not a desert temple chest, and you've probably never actually opened a real one. That's because this actually isn't a desert temple. In the files, it's called a desert pyramid. But they're actually is a separate real desert temple. That's because any village house with a brewing stand is technically called a temple. Most notably, this one in the plains village. And out of all the temples in all the villages, only one has a chest. This desert temple. And the chest is actually pretty decent.

Slimes spawn in swamps based on the moon phase. During a full moon, you'll get the most slimes, while a new moon, none can spawn. They also can't spawn above layer 70, but can spawn in areas of light level seven and below, unlike every other hostile mob that only spawns at zero. This means if you turn down your render distance and cover a swamp in redstone torches and any high area in regular torches, only slimes can spawn, meaning you'll get way more way quicker and fully optimized for the best nights before the moon changes.

Did you know if you kill a zombie piglin in one shot, it won't alert those nearby? But you need smite for this, so no one's really doing it. If you accidentally punch a zombie piglin, it'll alert all those nearby to attack until you're 60 blocks away from any or don't have a line of sight for enough time. But make sure your simulation distance is up cuz if you unload them, they don't get reset. If you accidentally punch a villager, it'll go tell all its friends and screw your prices. So, you either have to trap him before he tells anyone or But you need to do it in secret cuz if any villager sees, they'll tell everyone. I guess that's actually how it works.

purposefully getting your villagers butchered by a zombie just to later cure them like you did some good deed like Clash Royale releasing an OP card so everyone buys it just to nerf it days later is one of the [music] most overpowered techniques in Minecraft. This is because once you cure them, their prices are discounted massively. Most trades going to one and enchanted books all going down by 25 emeralds. And they keep the same trades as before they were zombified as long as you complete one trade with them before. So just let some zombies in and become the hero.

Do you ever notice new Minecraft animals just don't drop anything? Besides the Nautilus, which has a 5% chance to drop a shell, no real world animal added to Minecraft has had a meaningful drop since the new fish in 2018, dropping fish. I think it's cuz needing to kill an animal is a bad look. So, if there's ever a new item, there's an alternative way to get it. But the funniest exception to this is string. Because if you're skipping nights early game, sometimes the easiest way to get some for your first bow or fishing rod is killing the cats around villages. They made cats give a useful

What do the different levels of bad omen in ominous bottles even do? Well, they give different levels of the bad omen effect, which then convert to different levels of the raid omen effect when you enter a village, or durations of the trial omen effect when you enter a trial chambers. For raids, anything above level one gives an extra wave to the raid, and each higher level [music] gives the raiders more enchanted weapons for the reward when beating it of a higher level of hero of the village, which then discounts your villagers by these amounts. For trial [music] chambers, the higher level just converts to more time with the trial omen effect needed for opening ominous vaults. So for most players, I'd just save the higher level ones for when you want to grind for a maze.

There's three places in Minecraft where mobs can't spawn. Mushroom Islands, the deep dark, and trial chambers. Now, obviously mobs can spawn here from spawners, but if you break all of them, even if you turn off the lights, no mobs will spawn. And since it's more than 64 blocks from the surface, no one can see your name tag, making it the perfect secret base, and it's already completely built for you. The only issue is if someone gets an explorer map from a cgrapher.

Trumpets have been added to Minecraft. Not in the form of the brass tubes the kids who really like Five Nights at Freddy's carry around, but this note blocks make different noises depending on what block is beneath them. And now if they're beneath copper. Now I have no talent, but ITMG made some cool stuff.

Oh, so am I. Wait. He walked all the way over there in the time it took me to fall back down.

Minecraft is about to change massively. If you've ever switched from Bedrock Edition to Java, you might have noticed it's super laggy. If you have a cheap laptop that's a few years old, Minecraft just doesn't run very well, and you probably have to download performance mods. This is literally the first thing I tell a new player to do, which is kind of stupid, and it's always been that way. Mojang is switching the game renderer to Vulcan, which means nothing to me. But basically, the game will run way smoother, like four times better. FPS and shader packs will be much smoother and faster. But the downside is some computers over 10 years old won't be supported and every old mod needs to be updated. But overall, I think this is great. Now, cheap laptops will run the game perfectly, and I feel like 12-year-old systems weren't doing that to begin

Sweet berries are the most underrated food in Minecraft cuz it's actually terrible as a food. Exactly equal to a cookie, but not even fun cuz it's a cookie. Now, you might think I'm going to say you can prevent fall damage with it, which is true, but so can a lot of things. It's actually really useful in PvP. If someone's chasing you, you can place a bunch since they slow you down and prevent you from jumping. They're functionally early game cobwebs, which are easier to break, but that's the trade-off for how easy it is to obtain. And pairing these with a lava bucket is deadly.

There's a new method of a super easy slime ball farm because of the golden dandelion. Normally, you have to make a farm in an underground slime chunk, a swamp that won't work if it's a new moon, or something involving the new potion of oozing. But with the golden dandelion, it's much simpler. Because of pandas, every baby panda has a 1 in300 chance of sneezing every second, and every sneeze has a 1 in700 chance of dropping a slime ball. And since with a golden dandelion you can keep it a baby forever, if you put a hopper beneath it, it'll produce a slime ball on average once every 58 hours and 20 minutes. Now this goes down to 5 hours with a weak panda cuz they sneeze more. And if you breed a bunch of these, they can be decent.

We may have just gotten a hint for the next Minecraft update. In a small pre-release for the upcoming baby mob drop, they made a tweak to the villager trade rebalance experiment that hasn't been touched in over a year. This experiment makes it so villagers only trade certain items based on their biome, like rare enchantments or diamond armor, including needing the rare jungle and swamp villagers for unbreaking and mending books. I like this change. I think it adds a fun challenge, but whenever anything makes the game harder, people complain. So, they've seemed hesitant to add it, but will it finally come this

How many diamonds do you need? You know, when [music] you first get Fortune 3 and feel like you can finally mine diamonds efficiently? Well, how many do you need for your whole world's existence? Full diamond tools into armor take 36. But to get netherite, since you probably need to duplicate your upgrade template, that's an additional 70 diamonds. And even if you throw in some extra armor trims, repairs, juke boxes, enchanting tables, you really only need like two stacks of diamonds unless you lose some in death. And even then, once you progress far enough, you can get all your tools and armor [music] from villagers.

Have you ever accidentally created a ghost item like this? This happens when you drop an item at the same time you switch slots. And we can actually use this to our advantage. If you have some blocks and drop them at the same time you switch to your off hand, it'll look like you just duplicated them, but you actually just created a stack of ghost blocks. You can now use them to scale walls, parkour around, or go through water without actually using any blocks. And it doesn't leave a trail to follow.

You can get any enchantment within 10 minutes of loading a new world if you know what you're doing. Load in and find a village and on your way get seven leather and 21 sugarcane. These are to make seven books to make lectern. So if your village has a library, you won't need it. Mine all the hay bales and trade them with a farmer's wheat trade, which you can easily get from a composter. If you don't have enough emeralds from that, you can do a Fletcher's stick trade. Then dig three blocks down in two holes like this. Place a lectern at the bottom and a villager will pathf find in each. Place blocks above them. Dig down here. Break these blocks and start cycling the lecterns while checking the enchantment trade.

I have a secret. Two months ago, Mojang invited me to come to Sweden and take part in Minecraft Live, the bannual show Mojang puts on to unveil the next game drop and whatever else is coming soon to Minecraft. And well, I took the long haul and it was worth it cuz I now know what the next Minecraft update is and you don't. Uh-huh. But you will if you tune in to Minecraft Live on Saturday at 1:00 p.m.

Minecraft PS3 Edition source code from 2014 just leaked to the public and people are huge fans of the legacy console editions which were made by a whole different game studio making it completely distinct to modern Bedrock Edition. This means we'll learn all the secrets of the code in the coming days but also people will likely create a PC port including online play and mods for a game that was previously never moddable. At least unless they get into some legal trouble. We'll have to wait and see.

Minecraft red flags. The obvious ones are not filling in creeper holes, not fully cutting down trees, breaking blocks around your house for materials. But for some hot takes, and this might be unpopular, but instantly taming like 10 wolves on a new world and not even naming them like you cannot care for all of them. Red flag. Eating either the second your hunger goes down half or waiting till it goes down to like four. Somewhere in here is the sweet spot. Now, this one I'm guilty of, but using Fulbright all the time, like I don't know at what point this is just cheating.

This machine uses a glitch to duplicate TNT infinitely, and Mojang is aware of it, but choosing not to fix it. Back in the day, when a TNT dupe was discovered, Mojang would quickly patch it in an infinite game of cat and mouse. Players would even try to hide new exploits from being discovered by Mojang so they weren't fixed. But around 1.13, Mojang gave up, and TNT dupers have been in the game ever since. But why? Well, a Minecraft dev explained a few years ago that they fill a slot in the game that no intended mechanic can. So, will we ever get a feature that does this without glitches, or will it stay this way forever?

Moshine just announced the next Minecraft Live, the banual stream where they'll reveal the next Minecraft update and other secret stuff. This also means we'll probably get the baby drop and golden dandelion 3 days after. And shortly after that, new snapshots for the summer drop. And since the spring drop was so small, literally, I think we're due for a bigger update. Historically, they've saved bigger stuff for summer and winter when more people are playing. But we won't know until Saturday, March 21st at [music] 1:00 p.m. Eastern.

If you place a sunflower in the Nether, it will always point to a Nether fortress, which is an actual true fact I've seen going around Tik Tok. But upon looking into it, this traces back to a few Tik Toks in 2021, most notably this one, where someone tests if this myth is fact or cap, where they place a single sunflower, and it eventually leads to a Nether fortress, and they determine it's a fact. But something I didn't mention earlier, sunflowers just point east always. So, it doesn't really help anything. It'll just eventually point to another fortress cuz the world is infinite.

A question I've asked for years has been answered by Jeb. The giant is a legendary mob, one of the first coded into Minecraft, essentially being a huge zombie, dealing way more damage and having way more health, but it was never fully implemented in survival. Despite this, it's been summonable forever for no reason. It can't even move except for in 2018 where it was given zombie AI, meaning it can move around and even had a baby variant and a chicken jockey variant. But they reverted it back after a month. So the question is why? I've been pondering this for years and Jeb finally responded on Blue Sky. Honestly, I don't know.

You have [music] never stumbled upon a blue axelottle. And not because it's rare, because it's impossible. But it wasn't always. [music] When they were first added in 1.1.7, this could happen naturally, albeit the [music] chances were low. But so are pink sheep, and stumbling upon those is super fun. But a month later in [music] 1.17.1, they changed to only spawn from breeding, which was how it worked in Bedrock Edition. But I really think it should have switched the other way. How many people ever even breed an Axelottle? And you'll never get the majestic experience of stumbling upon it randomly.

The locator bar shows a square icon if someone's within 130 blocks of you. If they're between 130 and 230, it's a circle. 230 [music] and 330, a small square. And above 330, just this cross. But that's not all the information you can get. If a dot randomly disappears, that means someone changed dimensions, usually to the Nether. And if you go in two and see that the icon changed, since one block in the Nether is eight in [music] the overworld, by converting these numbers, you can better pinpoint their exact overworld distance. And if they're not in the Nether, you know they went to the end.

Remember that one time when you were a kid and tried to make an end portal in Creative and it didn't work? Well, that's cuz you stood in one spot and placed all the frames. What you had to do is stand in the middle and place them around you so they're all facing the right direction. But if you place three frames like this, then three frames on each side facing inward, and then three frames on top facing the other direction. You probably think this is one of those clickbait videos. Filling all of them does this. I'm not even joking. This is real. And then it got patched in 2024.

This is the most suspicious block in Minecraft and also one of the rarest. Suspicious sand or gravel spawn in ocean ruins, trail ruins, desert temples, and desert wells. And I wanted to find which suspicious block drop was the rarest. And while there's a ton of low drop rate items and trail ruins, there's so many gravel spawns, and so do desert temples and ocean ruins for how many unique items they get. That leaves the well, which each only get two suspicious sand. And with this item having a 1 in8 chance, you'll need four wells on average. A suspicious sand carrying suspicious stew.

There's a new useful item and more new textures in [music] the latest snapshot. We got new baby models for the dolphin squid, glow squid, turtle, and axelottles, which are really cute, but I think the turtle needs to be even smaller. Then we got the golden dandelion, which is a golden dandelion, but it [music] doesn't spawn naturally. You craft it with gold nuggets and then feed it to a baby mob to keep it a baby forever or until you feed it another one and it can grow

Have you ever had to dig up from a cave and been like, "Why is this taking so long?" Well, if you click F3 and F6, you can turn on the height map, which is a bunch of random letters and numbers. But this literally just tells you how high the surfaces. The O will show where the ocean floor is if you're in one or any water, and the ML will show the surface minus leaves. So, these are probably the most useful. But if you're ever in a cave trying to decide where to dig up, you can use this to find the perfect spot and then you'll know exactly how long it'll

We just got new baby models for the zombie husk, drowned piglin, zombie piglin, villager, and zombie villager. And that includes all the villager variants. This is what they look like before, and this is what they look like now. I'm most surprised about the baby zombie change cuz of how iconic the chicken jockey just became. But it kind of fits the movie with the huge head. Also, the hitbox is much bigger now, fully encompassing the head, so it's not hard to hit like the old one. But also, it can't fit through one block gaps now. They're so much less scary. And I hate it.

We have some new rarest mobs in Minecraft. That's because of the golden dandelion, a new item that when fed to a baby animal keeps it a baby forever. That brings us to the baby squid, glow squid, dolphin, and polar bear. All of which have babies, but can't breed, meaning they only spawn naturally during world generation, and you have to find one and feed it a golden dandelion before it grows up. Polar bears are easier because they spawn as babies more commonly, but specifically baby glow squids and dolphins are super rare.

Pixelmon or Cobblemon. These are two of the biggest mods ever, replicating Pokemon in Minecraft, and I wanted to play recently, so did some research. Pixelmon has been around much longer and is what I grew up playing. It has tons of tried and tested features, and every Pokemon 3D modeled. It's just the classic experience. Cobblemon is more focused towards aesthetics. The Pokémon are made to fit a Minecraft theme with very nice intricate animations. And really, the whole mod just looks better. But it is missing some core features like gyms, breeding, and so on. But Cobblemon's catching up, and I think it'll soon surpass Pixelmon in every way besides nostalgia cuz the style's way better. But I didn't want to settle for less features, so went with the Cobble Verse mod of Cobblemon, which pretty much adds every feature, and it's been great so far. So that's my recommendation.

I've been getting tagged in this Tik Tok of someone buying these Minecraft Legos. And is is that me? What am I doing there? No, this isn't a sponsorship. So, what is going on? Well, from the writing down here, I can tell it's some sort of Slavic language. But by googling the brand name, I found these My World sets, which are Chinese knockoffs of Minecraft sets, until I realized this says My Word, not My World. And upon further digging, I found some My Word sets listed on a Russian website. So, mystery solved, right? Well, then I found the My Word logo was a bit different than my box and still had Chinese branding. So, I finally found the exact company, which was listed in Ukrainian currency. And then, oh my god, it's me. Why am I the explorer? So, it's a Ukrainian knockoff of a Russian knockoff of a Chinese knockoff of Minecraft Legos. Okay, someone has
